'''Ed Chamberlain''' (born 8 February 1996) is an [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward or {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]], on a season-long loan from [[Hull F.C.]].

He has featured for a number of clubs throughout his career, and played as a {{rlp|wg}}er and {{rlp|fb}} earlier in his career.

==Background==
Chamberlain was born in [[Warrington]], [[Cheshire]], England.

==Career==
He has previously played for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] at [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], [[Workington Town]] in [[2017 League 1|League 1]] and [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in the [[Super League]]. Chamberlain has also spent time on loan from [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] at [[London Broncos|London]] during the [[2019 RFL Championship season]].

He has been dual-registered with [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="Misery for Whitehaven as London Broncos run riot">{{cite web|url=http://www.newsandstar.co.uk/sport/Misery-for-Whitehaven-as-London-Broncos-run-riot-6c1a7bc5-23e5-4f77-a121-79b16eb99ef8-ds|title=Misery for Whitehaven as London Broncos run riot|publisher=News & Star|date=2016-05-23|accessdate=2016-09-13}}</ref>

===Leigh===
On 20 October 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 Oct 2021|accessdate=26 Dec 2021|title=Leigh sign Ireland international ahead of Championship return|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-sign-ireland-international-ahead-of-championship-return/}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, Chamberlain played for Leigh in their [[2022 RFL 1895 Cup]] final victory over [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-1895-cup-final-defeat-to-leigh-centurions-gives-brian-mcdermotts-side-signal-of-their-progress-3713313/|title=Featherstone Rovers: 1895 Cup final defeat to Leigh Centurions gives Brian McDermott's side signal of their progress|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>
On 12 August 2023, Chamberlain played for Leigh in their 17-16 Challenge Cup final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers. It was Leigh's first major trophy in 52 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Chamberlain played 16 games for Leigh in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fifth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. Chamberlain played in their elimination playoff loss against Hull Kington Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://leighleopards.co.uk/leigh-leopards-2023-in-review/#:~:text=Overall%20playing%20record%3A,For%20694%2C%20Against%20580./|title=Leigh Leopards 2023 season in review|website=www.leighleopards.co.uk}}</ref>

===Hull F.C. (loan)===
On 23 April 2024 it was reported that he had signed for Hull F.C. in the Super League on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=23 Apr 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Leigh Leopards centre Ed Chamberlain makes Super League loan move|url=https://www.totalrl.com/leigh-leopards-centre-ed-chamberlain-makes-super-league-loan-move/}}</ref>
On 16 Jul 2024 it was reported that he had rejoined [[Hull FC]] on loan for the remainder of the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=16 Jul 2024|accessdate=17 Jul 2024|title=Hull FC loan Chamberlain for rest of the season|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cd16777ee74o}}</ref>

===Hull F.C.===
On 9 July 2024 it was reported that he had signed for Hull F.C. in the Super League on a permanent basis.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=9 Jul 2024|accessdate=3 Oct 2024|title=Leigh's Chamberlain and Holmes to join Hull FC next season|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cxx2jr5yyeeo}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls (loan)===
On 11 November 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]] on season-long loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=11 Nov 2025|accessdate=12 Nov 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=Hull FC’s Ed Chamberlain makes season-long loan move to Bradford Bulls|url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-fcs-ed-chamberlain-makes-season-long-loan-move-to-bradford-bulls/}}</ref>

===International===
He was named in the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.sbs.com.au/news/article/2017/10/07/ireland-name-rugby-league-world-cup-squad |title=Ireland name Rugby League World Cup squad |publisher=sbs.com.au |date=7 October 2017 |accessdate=7 October 2017}}</ref>
